name: Eurofins Cyber Security
description_en: Specialised IoT security laboratory within the Groningen knowledge ecosystem; contributes to the digital resilience of critical devices and industrial systems in accordance with NIS2.

name: Warpnet
description_en: Regional cybersecurity anchor in the North Netherlands with a focus on government clients and critical infrastructure; ESRF-relevant due to its 24/7 SOC and the development of digital resilience in the region.

name: ODC-Noord / SSO-Noord
description_en: Vital national government ICT infrastructure in the north of the Netherlands; a prime example of critical infrastructure requiring protection and resilience measures as envisaged by ESRF objectives.

name: Cyber Security Noord
description_en: Key organisation in the North Netherlands cybersecurity ecosystem; connects ESRF-relevant parties and strengthens collaboration between government, business, and education in the field of digital resilience.

name: Novar
description_en: Concrete solution for grid congestion via private energy networks; increases energy security for industrial and potentially critical customers in the region, essential for national resilience infrastructure.

name: New Energy Coalition / EDIH Noord-Nederland
description_en: Facilitator of energy innovation in the North Netherlands; relevant to ESRF due to its focus on energy resilience, smart grids, and hydrogen as a carrier of critical energy infrastructure.

name: DDFR (Data- en Dienstverlening Friesland)
description_en: Critical digital infrastructure in the North Netherlands (Noord-IX), NIS2-compliant; serves as the backbone for digital resilience of governments and vital sectors in Friesland.

name: Windpark Fryslân
description_en: Critical regional energy infrastructure with strategic importance for the energy independence of the North Netherlands; subject to NIS2 supervision as an essential energy supplier.

name: Cyber Safety Noord-Nederland
description_en: Regional platform for SME cyber resilience in the North Netherlands; supports smaller suppliers to vital sectors in meeting NIS2 requirements and ESRF-relevant resilience standards.

name: Veiligheidscampus Assen
description_en: National hub for resilience training and security-related education in the North Netherlands; makes a direct contribution to trained capacity for crisis management and national resilience.

name: DICTU (Dienst ICT Uitvoering)
description_en: Vital link in the digital infrastructure of the Dutch central government; manages systems for critical government tasks and contributes to the cybersecurity of the public domain.

name: Dutch TechZone / Greenwise Campus Emmen
description_en: Innovation platform for dual-use technologies in Drenthe; supports R&D at the intersection of industry and security, relevant to the resilience agenda and the security supply chain.

name: Port of Zwolle
description_en: Designated as a strategic logistics port; vital for mobility routes along the eastern corridor towards Germany; directly relevant to ESRF logistics and resilience themes.

name: NFIR (No Frills Incident Response)
description_en: Recognised cybersecurity specialist with CCV certification; directly relevant to the digital resilience of government and critical infrastructure in the IJsselland region and nationally.

name: Heras
description_en: Leading supplier of perimeter security for critical locations such as ports, data centres, secure government sites, and energy infrastructure; an essential component of the physical security layer of national resilience.

name: Thales Nederland
description_en: Core supplier to Dutch security services and international partners; dual-use technologies (radar, cyber, C2 systems) with direct applications in national resilience and security relevance to the ESRF.net community.

name: Height Technologies
description_en: Pioneer in Dutch drone and counter-drone technology; direct supplier to government security services and active member of NIDV; embodies dual-use innovation within the Twente security cluster.

name: Demcon Security
description_en: Versatile dual-use systems developer with direct government security contracts; the E-Lighter demonstrates how civilian high-tech innovation contributes to operational resilience and capability.

name: Aeronamic
description_en: Critical supplier for the European F-35 fleet and security aviation; a long-term maintenance contract for F-35 PTMS systems makes it an indispensable link in European air security capability.

name: RJ Safety & Security
description_en: Regional security partner for advanced technology companies such as Thales and the technology cluster in Twente; provides physical security for critical production and R&D facilities in the region.

name: Novel-T
description_en: Innovation driver of the Twente security cluster; connects university spin-offs and start-ups with security challenges through the Oost NL network and the Technology Base Enschede ecosystem.

name: Technology Base Enschede
description_en: Unique national test facility for dual-use security technology; springboard for security innovation in Twente and incubator for companies such as Height Technologies and AnGard Microwave.

name: DotSafe Cyber Security
description_en: Regional cybersecurity partner for SMEs and suppliers in the Twente security cluster; strengthens the digital resilience of the supply chain of companies such as Thales and Demcon.

name: Rheinmetall Nederland
description_en: Leading security technology supplier on the Veluwe with direct contracts for Dutch and international security organisations; the Boxer programme is a core capability of European land security forces.

name: Oost NL – Veiligheid & Innovatieprogramma
description_en: Key connector in the East Netherlands security ecosystem, representing over 20% of national security market turnover; ESRF-relevant as an organisation linking dual-use innovation and resilience.

name: Alliander / Liander
description_en: Vital grid operator for one third of the Netherlands; directly relevant to ESRF themes of energy resilience, NIS2 compliance for critical infrastructure, and grid congestion solutions.

name: Allego
description_en: Critical EV charging infrastructure supporting energy-mobility security; relevant to future electrification of heavy-duty vehicle fleets and emergency services.

name: Schipper Security
description_en: Certified security partner for government, security-sensitive sites, and critical infrastructure in the Gelderland region; innovative drone security enhances the resilience of vital locations.

name: Radboud Universiteit Nijmegen – Cybersecurity Onderzoeksgroep
description_en: Internationally recognised cybersecurity research centre; delivers fundamental and applied research that directly contributes to national digital resilience and the security practices of government and industry.

name: Joulz
description_en: Concrete evidence of energy resilience innovation in the Gelderland-Zuid region; the Tiel energy hub is a replicable model for strengthening the resilience of business parks and strategic logistics hubs.

name: HAN University of Applied Sciences
description_en: Academic institution delivering cybersecurity talent and practice-oriented research to the region; a relevant partner for ESRF-aligned knowledge development and human capital for digital resilience.

name: VABO Composites
description_en: Direct supplier for the new Royal Netherlands maritime frigates; represents Flevoland's business community at the LOI signing; embodies the region's dual-use composites innovation capability.

name: Secwatch
description_en: Experienced penetration testing specialist and SOC provider with security relevance; an active player in the Flevoland security innovation ecosystem, contributing to the digital resilience of government and vital sectors.

name: Windpark Zeewolde
description_en: Kritieke energieproductie-infrastructuur in Flevoland; enorme bijdrage aan nationale energieonafhankelijkheid en resilience; valt onder NIS2 als essentiële energieproductiefaciliteit.

name: Security Delta (HSD)
description_en: Central hub for the Dutch security ecosystem; essential to ESRF.net as a gateway to the entire ecosystem of security innovators, investors, and government partners.

name: Fox-IT (NCC Group)
description_en: Critical link in the Dutch cyber resilience chain; its trusted position with government and security services makes this company a high-value asset in any ESRF.net community focused on national cyber resilience.

name: TNO Safety & Security
description_en: Knowledge base of the Dutch security and resilience sector; TNO innovations form the technological foundation for resilience initiatives that ESRF.net aims to connect and scale.

name: Fugro
description_en: Essential for the protection of subsea critical infrastructure (cables, pipelines, offshore energy); highly relevant to ESRF.net given the growing hybrid threats to maritime infrastructure.

name: Cybersprint (Darktrace Company)
description_en: Innovative cybersecurity technology for attack surface management; as part of Darktrace, it illustrates the consolidation trend in the sector that ESRF.net support can accelerate.

name: Capgemini Netherlands (Cybersecurity)
description_en: Key player in the digitalisation of Dutch government and EU cybersecurity; major government contracts and international security involvement make this company relevant to ESRF.net community themes around government IT.

name: Siemens Nederland (Energiezekerheid & Security-IT)
description_en: Dual role in energy security (offshore wind) and government IT; a strategic partner for the energy transition and public sector modernisation that ESRF.net aims to support.

name: NFIR B.V.
description_en: Operational cyber resilience partner for vital sectors; CERT accreditation and a focus on critical infrastructure make NFIR relevant to ESRF.net listed organisations in the response layer of cyber resilience.

name: ENCS – European Network for Cyber Security
description_en: Unique European non-profit for cybersecurity in energy networks; the ENCS network of DSOs and regulators provides a direct connection to the critical infrastructure protection that ESRF.net supports.

name: Secura (Bureau Veritas Cybersecurity)
description_en: Recognised partner of the Dutch government and security sector for cybersecurity audits and certification; CREST and Common Criteria accreditations position Secura as a high-quality component of the national cyber resilience infrastructure.

name: Havenbedrijf Rotterdam (Port of Rotterdam Authority)
description_en: The Port of Rotterdam handles 45% of European cargo traffic and forms an indispensable link in the European supply chain and strategic logistics; directly relevant to ESRF.net as a community for critical infrastructure resilience.

name: FERM Zeehavens
description_en: Model organisation for public-private cyber resilience collaboration in critical infrastructure; FERM's approach to collective cyber resilience closely aligns with ESRF.net's mission of innovative collaboration and PPP models.

name: Stedin Netbeheer
description_en: Operator of critical energy networks in the Randstad heartland of the Netherlands; Stedin's energy transition investments and OT cybersecurity are directly relevant to ESRF.net's focus on energy security and critical infrastructure.

name: Damen Shipyards Group
description_en: Core supplier of maritime capability to the Netherlands and international partners; Damen's vessels and patrol ships directly contribute to maritime security and national resilience that ESRF.net supports.

name: Royal HaskoningDHV
description_en: Strategic engineering partner for national resilience programmes, water safety, and energy transition; RHDHV's broad critical infrastructure portfolio coverage makes it relevant for ESRF.net community focuss in infrastructure resilience.

name: Portbase
description_en: Critical digital infrastructure for Europe's largest port; Portbase's role in quantum security testing and digital port resilience directly aligns with ESRF.net's focus on innovative critical infrastructure security.

name: KPN (Cybersecurity & Kritieke Telecominfrastructuur)
description_en: Backbone of the Dutch digital infrastructure; KPN's investments in sovereign cloud, quantum communications, and critical infrastructure security are core components of the national digital resilience that ESRF.net aims to strengthen.

name: Deltalinqs
description_en: Umbrella organisation of the Rotterdam mainport ecosystem; Deltalinqs' role as a connecting link between business and government on safety and resilience makes it relevant to ESRF.net's public-private partnership model.

name: Solvinity
description_en: Operator of the Netherlands' most critical digital government infrastructure (DigiD); the sovereignty debate surrounding Solvinity illustrates precisely the type of vulnerability that ESRF.net aims to address through European community collaboration.

name: EclecticIQ
description_en: European market leader in cyber threat intelligence platforms for government and security organisations; directly applicable to ESRF.net listed organisations and public partners strengthening national cyber resilience.

name: AMS-IX (Amsterdam Internet Exchange)
description_en: One of Europe's most critical digital infrastructure hubs; an outage at AMS-IX would have direct consequences for national digital resilience — making it a core asset within the ESRF.net universe.

name: ThreatFabric
description_en: Protector of financial critical infrastructure; the resilience of the European financial system is a core component of national security and aligns with ESRF.net's focus on critical infrastructure protection.

name: Secura (Amsterdam-vestiging)
description_en: See also Haaglanden entry; Secura's Amsterdam headquarters strengthens its presence in the two largest security regions and is essential for critical infrastructure protection supported by ESRF.net.

name: IBM Netherlands (Security IT & Cybersecurity)
description_en: Strategic partner for the digitalisation of Dutch government; the GrIT infrastructure represents one of the Netherlands' largest government IT investments and directly aligns with ESRF.net's security strengthening theme.

name: Maritiem Cluster Den Helder
description_en: Strategic hub for maritime security and offshore energy security; the combination of Royal Netherlands maritime capability and offshore wind farm operations makes Den Helder a key location for ESRF.net's maritime and energy resilience community focuss.

name: Marshall Netherlands
description_en: Part of the South Holland aviation cluster delivering critical capacity for security maintenance; relevant to ESRF.net's community focuss in the European security industrial base.

name: Northwave Cyber Security
description_en: One of the largest and most certified Dutch cybersecurity service providers; Northwave's SOC services for vital infrastructure and government make it a key player in national cyber resilience relevant to ESRF.net.

name: Royal HaskoningDHV (Hoofdkantoor)
description_en: See also Rotterdam entry; the Amersfoort headquarters coordinates the broad resilience advisory portfolio that is essential for ESRF.net community focuss in water safety and climate resilience.

name: Damen Group (Hoofdkantoor)
description_en: Global maritime shipbuilder with international security contracts; the production of frigates and patrol vessels in Gorinchem constitutes a direct contribution to European maritime security capability relevant to ESRF.net.

name: Solvinity (Huizen-locatie)
description_en: See also Rotterdam-Rijnmond entry; Solvinity's Huizen facility is the operational site for critical government IT — the sovereignty debate makes this directly relevant to ESRF.net's European digital independence theme.

name: Liander (Alliander)
description_en: Regional grid operator for critical energy infrastructure in the Zaanstreek and surrounding area; grid congestion challenges and investments in smart networks are directly relevant to ESRF.net's energy security theme.

name: ASML
description_en: Strategic dual-use asset; critical technology for Western semiconductor sovereignty and security electronics. Export-control-classified technology, essential for chip production in the security sector. The city of Eindhoven is acquiring the BIC campus on national security grounds.

name: VDL Groep
description_en: Designated the 'new flagship of the Dutch security industry' by Minister Brekelmans (Oct 2025). Strategic manufacturing platform for Dutch and Ukrainian security requirements. 10-year government contract, 120,000 m² production facility in Born.

name: KMWE Precision / KMWE Aerospace
description_en: Essential component of the F-35 supply chain and the broader international aerospace supply network. Embodies the dual-use manufacturing expertise of the Brainport ecosystem.

name: Teledyne Adimec
description_en: Core supplier of surveillance and intelligence camera solutions for the security and border protection sector. MIL-STD-certified products for critical security infrastructure.

name: Thales Cryogenics B.V.
description_en: Critical security technology supplier for IR sensor technology and space surveillance systems. Part of the Thales security pillar serving European partner states.

name: Secura B.V. (Bureau Veritas Cybersecurity)
description_en: NIDV-registered cybersecurity supplier for Dutch government security. Specialised in OT/ICS security, critical infrastructure, and government IT. CCV pentest certified.

name: DeltaQuad (Vertical Technologies)
description_en: Direct government contract holder (Oct 2025). Actively deployed in the Ukraine conflict. Core element of the Dutch unmanned systems ecosystem based in Brainport.

name: DeepTechXL
description_en: Essential financing platform for dual-use start-ups in the Brainport security ecosystem. International security accelerator partner and member of BITS. Article 9 ESG-compliant fund.

name: Brainport Development / BITS-programma
description_en: Government programme (BITS) accelerating dual-use innovation in Brabant. Host of an international security accelerator node. Central link in the Dutch security innovation ecosystem.

name: StandardAero B.V.
description_en: Critical maintenance partner for RNLAF aircraft engines at an active air base. Essential for the operational readiness of Dutch air security services.

name: Chromalloy Holland B.V.
description_en: Specialised repair of critical turbine components for aviation. Part of the global Chromalloy group, which maintains aviation engines for US and international security partners.

name: SABIC Bergen op Zoom
description_en: BRZO-classified critical chemical infrastructure. Strategic materials producer for security supply chains (thermoplastics for heavy-duty vehicles and electronics).

name: BOM (Brabantse Ontwikkelings Maatschappij)
description_en: Manages a €100M SecFund for security innovation. Central financing and development partner in the Brabant security ecosystem (BITS). Essential for scaling dual-use start-ups.

name: EBN Veiligheidsdienst
description_en: Specialised port security provider for the Moerdijk chemical complex. Critical security partner for BRZO-classified industrial infrastructure in West Brabant.

name: Acecore Technologies
description_en: Dutch manufacturer of professional heavy-lift UAVs with proven deployment in active conflict zones (Ukraine). Contribution to mine detection operations strengthens resilience in high-risk areas.

name: Tulip Tech
description_en: Direct government contract holder (Oct 2025) for the production of high-performance drone batteries. Core supplier in the Dutch drone ecosystem centred on VDL Born.

name: Vanderlande Industries
description_en: World leader in airport security and baggage screening systems. Critical supplier for the security of international transport infrastructure.

name: Hoppenbrouwers Techniek
description_en: KIWA-certified installation company for fire protection and security systems at critical industrial infrastructure. Active at government and security-related sites in Brabant-Noord.

name: North Sea Port
description_en: Vital maritime port infrastructure (3rd in EU by cargo value). ISPS-certified. Strategic import hub for energy (LNG), chemical raw materials and security logistics.

name: DeltaSafe Security
description_en: ISPS-certified maritime port security provider for the Zeeland port area. Essential for the physical security of the vital North Sea port infrastructure.

name: Safety & Security Zeeland B.V.
description_en: Specialised ISPS port security provider for the strategic Zeeland port complex (North Sea Port). Contributes to the physical maritime security chain.

name: Milrem Robotics (productie bij VDL Born)
description_en: Direct government contract holder (Oct 2025). Series production of operationally proven unmanned ground vehicles in South Limburg. Contributes to European autonomous systems capabilities.

name: Chemelot Industrial Park / Chemelot Site
description_en: The Netherlands' largest BRZO complex. Vital supplier of basic chemicals for the Dutch economy. Own fire brigade (180 personnel) = autonomous resilience capacity. Recently subject to drone reconnaissance threats.

name: Sitech Services
description_en: Operational safety core of the largest BRZO complex in the Netherlands. Manages its own fire brigade, TETRA communications, and Chemelot's safety infrastructure. Essential for national resilience.

name: Heijmans N.V.
description_en: Builder and operator of critical energy and transport infrastructure. Dedicated cybersecurity function for vital infrastructure processes. Active in government accommodation and security-related projects.

name: Enexis Groep (regio Limburg-Noord)
description_en: Operator of critical energy distribution networks in Limburg supplying government security facilities (VDL Born, AFNORTH Brunssum) and vital industrial complexes. Essential for national energy resilience.

name: OCI Nitrogen
description_en: BRZO-classified producer of ammonia (hazardous substance) at Chemelot. Special statutory covenant with the state for ammonia transport. Critical supplier of fertilisers for European food security.

name: DSM-Firmenich (Nutritional Products, Geleen)
description_en: Producer of vitamins and nutritional ingredients at Chemelot — strategically important for food security and the medical supply chain. Part of Chemelot's vital industrial cluster.

name: Thales Belgium
description_en: Thales Belgium employs over 1,200 people across nine sites, delivering security electronics, 70mm rocket systems, C4ISR solutions, cryptography, and cybersecurity services to international partners and Belgian security services. As a permanent presence in Brussels, it directly serves European institutions and major security headquarters.

name: imec
description_en: imec is the world's leading independent semiconductor research and innovation centre, developing next-generation chip technologies critical for secure communications, autonomous security systems, and Europe's technological sovereignty. Backed by a €2.5 billion EU Chips Act investment, imec is Europe's foremost microelectronics research hub.

name: Port of Antwerp-Bruges
description_en: The Port of Antwerp-Bruges is Europe's second-largest seaport and a critical node in European supply chains, handling over 280 million tonnes of cargo annually. Its advanced security operations—including AI surveillance, drone monitoring, and the pioneering Port 2 Port Security Platform—set a European benchmark for critical infrastructure protection.

name: Intigriti
description_en: Intigriti is Europe's leading bug bounty and crowdsourced security platform, connecting over 125,000 vetted ethical hackers with organisations seeking to identify vulnerabilities before malicious actors do. With EU data sovereignty, high-grade encryption, and CREST accreditation, it serves hundreds of organisations across Europe.

name: NVISO
description_en: NVISO is a Belgian cybersecurity firm specialising in threat intelligence, incident response, penetration testing, and security operations centre services for critical infrastructure operators, government agencies, and financial institutions across Europe. It is widely recognised as one of Belgium's top cybersecurity consultancies.

name: Proximus
description_en: Proximus is Belgium's leading telecommunications operator and a key provider of secure connectivity, managed security services, and critical communications infrastructure for government and enterprise clients. Through its security division, it delivers network protection, SIEM services, and NIS2 compliance solutions across Belgium.

name: Sweepatic
description_en: Sweepatic is a Belgian cybersecurity scale-up specialising in continuous automated attack surface management, helping organisations discover and manage their exposed digital assets before adversaries do. It is recognised among Belgium's top cybersecurity companies and serves large enterprises and public sector clients.

name: RHEA Group
description_en: RHEA Group is a Belgian-founded engineering and cybersecurity company supporting European space agencies, international security organisations, and critical infrastructure operators with advanced security solutions, systems engineering, and risk management. It operates across Europe and is a recognised partner of ESA and the European Union.

name: Secutec
description_en: Secutec is a Belgian managed security services provider offering 24/7 threat detection, incident response, and cybersecurity consulting to businesses and public organisations. It is a member of the Belgian Cyber Security Coalition and provides security operations centre services aligned with NIS2 requirements.

name: Toreon
description_en: Toreon is a Belgian cybersecurity consultancy specialising in security architecture, penetration testing, threat modelling, and privacy compliance. It helps organisations build security by design and supports critical infrastructure operators in achieving GDPR and NIS2 compliance.

name: Space Applications Services
description_en: Space Applications Services is a Belgian company providing advanced space mission systems, ground control software, and real-time data services for ESA, international security organisations, and critical infrastructure protection programmes. It participates in European security fund projects including mine countermeasure and maritime surveillance systems.

name: CMB (Compagnie Maritime Belge)
description_en: CMB is one of Belgium's oldest and most prominent maritime groups, operating a global fleet of bulk carriers, container ships, and chemical tankers from its Antwerp base. As a major stakeholder in Antwerp port operations and a pioneer in hydrogen-powered shipping, CMB contributes to the security and resilience of critical maritime supply chains.

name: Thales Alenia Space Belgium
description_en: Thales Alenia Space Belgium is the world leader in satellite power conditioning and distribution, and Europe's sole automated manufacturer of satellite photovoltaic assemblies. Operating across facilities in Charleroi, Leuven, and Hasselt, it provides critical space electronics for Ariane 5 and 6 launchers and global telecommunications satellites.

name: Engie Belgium
description_en: Engie Belgium operates the country's nuclear power plants at Doel and Tihange, which together account for approximately half of Belgium's electricity production. As a critical energy infrastructure operator, Engie Belgium plays a central role in Belgian energy security and the country's transition toward resilient renewable energy.

name: ASTRID
description_en: ASTRID operates Belgium's national emergency and security communications network, providing secure digital radio communications for police, fire services, emergency medical services, and other critical public safety agencies across the country. It is a foundational component of Belgium's national security infrastructure.

name: HENSOLDT AG
description_en: HENSOLDT is a leading European security electronics company headquartered near Munich, specializing in sensor solutions for security applications including radar, optronics, electronic warfare, and avionics. The company achieved record order intake of €4.71 billion in 2025 and supplies critical sensor systems to international security partners.

name: Airbus Security & Space
description_en: Airbus Security and Space is the security and space division of Airbus, headquartered in Toulouse, developing and manufacturing tactical aircraft (A400M, A330 MRTT), government satellites, surveillance systems, and working on next-generation programs including the FCAS Future Combat Air System and Eurodrone. It is the leading security industrial supplier of the French Procurement Agency (DGA).

name: ATLAS ELEKTRONIK GmbH
description_en: ATLAS ELEKTRONIK is a world-leading naval electronics and sonar systems company headquartered in Bremen, operating as part of TKMS. With over 120 years of experience, the company develops integrated sonar systems, submarine combat management systems, heavyweight torpedoes (SeaHake), mine warfare systems, unmanned underwater vehicles, and coastal surveillance systems for more than 40 navies worldwide.

name: OHB SE
description_en: OHB SE is Europe's third-largest space company, headquartered in Bremen, and a prime contractor for German government satellite reconnaissance systems SAR-Lupe and SARah. The company builds the Galileo navigation satellites and is involved in Earth observation, telecommunications, and space exploration.

name: RENK Group AG
description_en: RENK Group AG is the world's leading manufacturer of mission-critical propulsion solutions for security and civil applications, headquartered in Augsburg. The company powers virtually every major tracked heavy-duty vehicle platform including the Leopard 2, Bradley, and Puma with its transmissions, and generates revenue across land, naval, and energy applications.

name: Rohde & Schwarz GmbH & Co. KG
description_en: Rohde & Schwarz is a leading global technology group headquartered in Munich specializing in secure communications, electronic warfare, radio monitoring, test & measurement, and cybersecurity. The company achieved net revenue of €3.16 billion in 2024/25 and holds major contracts with government security agencies across Europe.

name: IABG mbH
description_en: IABG (Industrieanlagen-Betriebsgesellschaft mbH) is a leading European independent testing and consulting firm founded in 1961, headquartered near Munich, providing technical and scientific services for security, aerospace, automotive, and space sectors. With 1,200 employees, IABG supports the German government and industry with complex systems testing, certification, and lifecycle management.

name: ESG Elektroniksystem- und Logistik-GmbH
description_en: ESG is a leading German company for the development, integration, and operation of complex security-relevant electronic and IT systems for government authorities and public security agencies, now part of the HENSOLDT Group. With 1,400+ employees, ESG operates as Germany's national technology and innovation partner for security-critical IT systems.

name: secunet Security Networks AG
description_en: secunet is Germany's leading cybersecurity company and the designated IT security partner of the Federal Republic of Germany, providing classified communications hardware (SINA), eGovernment, and critical infrastructure protection solutions. With over 1,000 experts and €406 million in 2024 revenue, secunet serves federal ministries, over 20 DAX companies, and major national/international organizations.

name: Siemens AG
description_en: Siemens AG is a global technology conglomerate headquartered in Munich and Europe's leading provider of operational technology (OT) security solutions for industrial and critical infrastructure environments. Siemens integrates cybersecurity capabilities across its industrial automation, control systems, and digital industries platforms, serving energy, utilities, manufacturing, transportation, and public infrastructure operators across Europe.

name: Infineon Technologies AG
description_en: Infineon Technologies AG is a global semiconductor leader headquartered in Munich, with its major Dresden fab producing security chips, power semiconductors, and automotive electronics critical to European supply chain sovereignty. The company is investing €5 billion in a new Smart Power Fab in Dresden (opening 2026), supported by €1 billion from EU Chips Act funding, reinforcing European technological independence.

name: Quantum Systems GmbH
description_en: Quantum Systems is a Munich-based drone unicorn (€1 billion+ valuation) developing electric, AI-powered autonomous surveillance drones for both security and civilian use. The company's flagship VECTOR drone is deployed by security services in Germany, Spain, the UK, Australia, Romania, the Philippines, and other countries.

name: ARX Robotics GmbH
description_en: ARX Robotics is a Munich-based security tech startup building Europe's leading autonomous unmanned ground vehicles (UGVs) for logistics, evacuation, surveillance, and support missions. The company opened Europe's largest security robotics production facility in the Munich metropolitan region and has deployed the largest contingent of Western-designed UGVs in Ukraine, backed by international security innovation funding.

name: G DATA CyberDefense AG
description_en: G DATA CyberDefense is one of Germany's oldest and most established cybersecurity companies, headquartered in Bochum, specializing in endpoint protection, EDR, and cybersecurity solutions for SMEs and enterprises under the 'Made in Germany' banner. The company is widely deployed across German industry and public sector organizations for GDPR-compliant, data-sovereign security.

name: Utimaco GmbH
description_en: Utimaco is a leading German manufacturer of Hardware Security Modules (HSMs) and cybersecurity appliances, headquartered in Aachen, serving banking, telecommunications, government, and IoT security sectors. Its BSI-certified hardware provides the cryptographic roots of trust for critical digital infrastructure including payment systems, PKI, and identity management.

name: SySS GmbH
description_en: SySS GmbH is Germany's gold-standard penetration testing firm, BSI-accredited and headquartered in Tübingen, trusted by government agencies, critical infrastructure operators, and automotive manufacturers. The company specializes in deep technical security assessments of complex web applications, embedded systems, automotive electronics, and OT/ICS environments.

name: T-Systems International GmbH
description_en: T-Systems is the enterprise ICT arm of Deutsche Telekom, headquartered in Frankfurt, operating one of Europe's largest Security Operations Centers (SOCs) analyzing billions of security events daily. The company provides end-to-end managed security services, cloud security, 5G security, and critical infrastructure protection for large enterprises and government clients across Germany and Europe.

name: Siemens Energy AG
description_en: Siemens Energy is a global energy technology company spun off from Siemens AG in 2020, specializing in power generation, transmission, and the security of critical energy infrastructure. The company provides OT cybersecurity solutions through its Omnivise T3000 control system and the Eos.ii AI monitoring platform, protecting gas turbines, wind farms, and grid infrastructure against cyberattacks.

name: MTU Aero Engines AG
description_en: MTU Aero Engines is Germany's leading aircraft engine manufacturer and Europe's largest independent engine company, headquartered in Munich. MTU designs and produces engine components for the Eurofighter Typhoon's EJ200 engine, the A400M's TP400-D6, and various helicopter platforms, contributing to European aerospace sovereignty and the FCAS next-generation aviation programme.

name: Liebherr-Aerospace & Transportation SAS (German division)
description_en: Liebherr Group's aerospace and security division, with major German operations centered on the Lindenberg and Kempten sites in Bavaria, produces flight control actuation systems, landing gear, and air management systems for aircraft including the Eurofighter Typhoon, A400M, and Tornado. Liebherr is a key supplier in the European aerospace industrial base.

name: Diehl Stiftung & Co. KG (Group)
description_en: Diehl Stiftung is the parent holding company of the Diehl Group, headquartered in Nuremberg, with business divisions spanning Diehl Security (IRIS-T missiles), Diehl Aviation, Diehl Metering, and Diehl Metal Applications. The group employs around 16,000 people globally and represents one of Germany's most significant dual-use industrial conglomerates in the security and technology sectors.

name: Rhebo GmbH
description_en: Rhebo is a Leipzig-based German OT/ICS security company, part of Landis+Gyr, specializing in anomaly detection and network monitoring for industrial control systems and critical energy infrastructure. The company's Rhebo Industrial Protector technology provides real-time visibility and threat detection for power grids, water utilities, and industrial facilities compliant with NIS2 and IEC 62443.

name: INFODAS GmbH
description_en: INFODAS is a German cybersecurity company headquartered in Cologne, specializing in secure IT solutions for the public sector, government security services, and critical infrastructure. The company develops certified cross-domain security products including security gateways and data diodes, along with ISMS consulting, risk analysis, cloud security, and penetration testing for government clients.

name: IABG – Acentiss GmbH
description_en: Acentiss is an IABG subsidiary in Ottobrunn near Munich specializing in engineering services for security aviation, unmanned systems, and autonomous platforms. The company develops the HADIS autonomous unmanned tactical resupply system for government clients and provides aerospace engineering and UAS development services.

name: Thales Deutschland GmbH
description_en: Thales Deutschland is the German subsidiary of the Franco-Dutch Thales Group, providing security electronics, command and control systems, maritime solutions, and secure communications for German government agencies and public security services. The company operates sites in Stuttgart, Ditzingen, Wilhelmshaven, and Kiel, serving the land, maritime, and air domains.

name: Bundesdruckerei GmbH
description_en: Bundesdruckerei is Germany's state-owned security printing and digital identity company, headquartered in Berlin, responsible for producing passports, ID cards, banknotes, and official security documents. The company also provides digital identity solutions, PKI infrastructure, and secure eGovernment services, making it a critical component of Germany's civil security and national identity infrastructure.

name: VMRay GmbH
description_en: VMRay is a German cybersecurity company headquartered in Bochum specializing in advanced malware analysis and threat intelligence through its AI-powered sandbox platform. VMRay's technology is trusted by government agencies, critical infrastructure operators, and enterprise security teams worldwide for detecting zero-day attacks, APT threats, and nation-state malware.

name: Airbus Helicopters Deutschland GmbH
description_en: Airbus Helicopters Deutschland, headquartered in Donauwörth, is the principal production and MRO center for helicopters in Germany, with a workforce of over 7,000. The site assembles the H145M helicopter and NH90, maintains the German government helicopter fleet, and develops next-generation urban air mobility concepts alongside aviation security programmes.

name: Frequentis AG (German operations)
description_en: Frequentis is an Austrian-headquartered company with significant German operations providing communication and information systems for safety-critical infrastructure including air traffic management, railway control, public safety, and coast guard/maritime rescue. The company's MARIS maritime communications system and ATM solutions are deployed across German and European critical infrastructure.

name: ZF Friedrichshafen AG
description_en: ZF Friedrichshafen is a leading global automotive and industrial technology group headquartered in Friedrichshafen, producing drive systems, chassis systems, and active safety technologies. ZF supplies heavy-duty vehicle transmissions and drive systems for armored platforms, and its advanced sensor and autonomous systems contribute to the dual-use security technology sector.

name: Bosch Global Software Technologies (Security Division)
description_en: Robert Bosch GmbH's security systems and software division, headquartered in Stuttgart, is a major provider of physical security solutions including video surveillance, access control, intrusion detection, and cybersecurity for buildings, critical infrastructure, and industrial facilities across Germany and Europe. Bosch's integrated security platforms serve airports, power plants, and government buildings.

name: Abeking & Rasmussen Schiffs- und Yachtwerft
description_en: Abeking & Rasmussen is a specialized shipyard in Lemwerder near Bremen developing and manufacturing mine countermeasure vessels, patrol boats, and special vessels up to 125 meters for maritime security forces. With 440 employees in the maritime business area, the company is an important supplier to the German and international clients for asymmetric threat and mine warfare capabilities.

name: Fassmer Werft GmbH & Co. KG
description_en: Fassmer is a German shipyard in Lower Saxony specializing in coast guard vessels, offshore patrol ships (OPV), search and rescue vessels, and naval modular systems. The company builds OPV-type blue-water patrol ships and naval warfare systems with flexible module designs for coast guard agencies and navies, employing 1,500 people worldwide.

name: DataGuard GmbH
description_en: DataGuard is a Munich-based privacy and information security compliance platform helping organizations across Germany and Europe implement GDPR, NIS2, ISO 27001, and TISAX requirements. The company's AI-driven platform automates privacy management, information security risk assessment, and compliance documentation for critical infrastructure operators and enterprises.

name: Continental AG (Automotive Resilience)
description_en: Continental AG is a leading global automotive and industrial technology company headquartered in Hanover, producing tires, vehicle safety systems, sensors, and electronic control systems including applications in heavy-duty vehicles and security mobility. Continental's run-flat tires and advanced vehicle systems are specified in various government security vehicle programmes.

name: Terma A/S
description_en: Terma is Denmark's largest security technology company, developing and manufacturing radar systems, air security C2 solutions, aircraft self-protection systems, aerostructures (F-35 gun pod), coastal surveillance radars, and counter-UAS systems. It holds a 30-year framework agreement with Danish government authorities as prime contractor for all air and missile security integration.

name: Weibel Scientific A/S
description_en: Weibel Scientific is the global leader in advanced Doppler radar systems with 40+ years of experience and over 5,000 systems delivered to 40+ countries. Its radars are used for ballistic target tracking, counter-UAS/SHORAD, artillery fire control, and test range instrumentation. Thales purchased 12 Weibel XENTA radars for its ground-based air security solution.

name: Heimdal Security A/S
description_en: Heimdal Security is a rapidly growing Danish cybersecurity company providing a unified AI-driven platform covering endpoint detection & response (EDR), threat intelligence, privileged access management, and email security. It serves government, critical infrastructure, and security sector clients, with solutions certified for use in security-critical environments.

name: Energinet
description_en: Energinet is Denmark's state-owned transmission system operator for both electricity and natural gas, managing the national high-voltage grid, international interconnectors, and gas import infrastructure including the Baltic Pipe from Norway. Following the Nord Stream sabotage, it implemented enhanced physical and cybersecurity measures for all critical infrastructure.

name: Centre for Cyber Security (CFCS) – Denmark
description_en: The Centre for Cyber Security (CFCS) is Denmark's national IT security authority under the Intelligence Service, responsible for protecting vital societal functions against cyber threats, advising public and private critical infrastructure operators, and operating the national cyber situational awareness center 24/7. Denmark ranks first in the 2025 FM Resilience Index for cybersecurity resilience.

name: Vestas Wind Systems A/S
description_en: Vestas is the world's leading wind turbine manufacturer, headquartered in Denmark, providing the hardware backbone for Europe's offshore and onshore wind energy buildout that is central to reducing dependency on Russian energy. Its turbines power critical energy infrastructure across Europe and its supply chain resilience is strategically significant for EU energy security.

name: Demant A/S – Oticon Medical (dual-use communications research)
description_en: While primarily a hearing technology group, Demant's signal processing and secure audio communications research capabilities have dual-use applications in security communications and noise-canceling technologies for personnel in critical environments. Denmark's advanced audio technology cluster contributes to secure personnel communications.

name: Nammo Elling A/S (re-established 2024)
description_en: In 2024, Nammo and the Danish government signed an agreement to re-establish ammunition production at the historic Elling plant near Frederikshavn, with groundbreaking in November 2024. This facility will produce ammunition for Danish and international security partners, strengthening Denmark's sovereign security production capacity and Nordic industrial cooperation.

name: CybExer Technologies
description_en: CybExer Technologies provides advanced cyber range and training platforms, enabling organizations to simulate and respond to sophisticated cyberattacks through live-fire exercises and capture-the-flag drills. The company's Cyber Range platform is used by government agencies, security services, and critical infrastructure operators including the Estonian Cyber Unit.

name: Guardtime
description_en: Guardtime is a global cybersecurity company pioneering blockchain-based data integrity solutions, with its KSI (Keyless Signature Infrastructure) blockchain deployed by governments and enterprises since 2008. The company has international security partnerships with the Estonian government and the European Space Agency to secure critical systems against tampering and unauthorized modification.

name: Threod Systems
description_en: Threod Systems develops, produces, and operates customized unmanned aerial systems (UAS) for surveillance and intelligence gathering for government, security, and civil applications. The company has delivered products to 25 countries including 8 international security partner states and operates a training and service center in Ukraine.

name: DefSecIntel Solutions
description_en: DefSecIntel is an Estonian security technology company developing AI-powered autonomous surveillance systems for land border, coastal, and critical infrastructure monitoring. The company's vertically integrated mobile autonomous towers with integrated drone stations provide AI-assisted, green-energy-powered surveillance solutions deployed in multiple international security partner countries.

name: Baltic Workboats
description_en: Baltic Workboats is an Estonian shipbuilder on Saaremaa island, building patrol vessels, pilot boats, search and rescue craft, and semi-autonomous maritime platforms. The company is leading a 23-partner, 10-country European Security Fund project to develop semi-autonomous maritime vessels for European coast guards and maritime security services.

name: Clarified Security
description_en: Clarified Security is an Estonian cybersecurity company specializing in penetration testing, red teaming, and hands-on cyber resilience training for government, enterprise, and critical infrastructure clients. The company is involved in high-stakes cyber security exercises including CCDCOE's LOCKED SHIELDS exercise.

name: KappaZeta
description_en: KappaZeta is an Estonian satellite remote sensing company specializing in radar satellite monitoring and AI-powered Earth observation analytics. The company provides dual-use intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance data derived from SAR satellite imagery to security, government, and environmental monitoring customers.

name: Navantia S.A.
description_en: Navantia is Spain's state-owned shipbuilder, constructing frigates (F-110 next-generation), S-80 submarines, and offshore patrol vessels for the Spanish maritime services and international customers. It operates major shipyards in Ferrol, Cartagena, and San Fernando, and is a strategic partner for international maritime security capability development.

name: Airbus Security & Space España (Getafe)
description_en: Airbus Security and Space Spain (headquartered in Getafe near Madrid) is the lead manufacturer of the A400M tactical transport, C295 tactical airlifter, and CN-235 patrol aircraft. Spain represents approximately 20% of the Airbus Security and Space ownership and is a key industrial partner for the FCAS next-generation combat aircraft program.

name: GMV Innovating Solutions S.L.
description_en: GMV is a leading Spanish security and space technology company providing navigation systems, satellite ground control software, cybersecurity solutions, and C4ISR platforms to government and space clients worldwide. It is a lead partner in the SATNUS FCAS Remote Carrier Technology Pillar and develops the EU cyber security training platform CDTEXP.

name: ITP Aero (Industria de Turbo Propulsores)
description_en: ITP Aero is Spain's aero engine leader, responsible for the EJ200 engine for the Eurofighter Typhoon, the TP400 for the A400M, and the MTR390 for the Tiger helicopter. It is Spain's prime for the FCAS propulsion system and invests ~€100M annually in R&T, including hydrogen propulsion and future combat air engine technologies.

name: SENER Aeroespacial
description_en: SENER Aeroespacial is a leading Spanish engineering firm active in security, aerospace, and space, providing structural components, actuation systems, and mission-critical engineering for satellite programs, the Eurofighter, A400M, and FCAS. As a co-founder of SATNUS, it leads Spain's contribution to the FCAS Remote Carrier Technology Pillar.

name: Telefónica Tech / Telefónica
description_en: Telefónica is Spain's national telecommunications operator and one of Europe's largest, operating critical communications infrastructure that underpins national security. Through Telefónica Tech, it provides cybersecurity services, cloud security, managed SOC services, and digital resilience solutions to Spanish and European government and security sector clients.

name: Enagas S.A.
description_en: Enagas is Spain's national gas transmission system operator and one of Europe's largest LNG operators, managing 12,000 km of gas pipelines and three LNG regasification plants. As Spain is Europe's gateway for LNG imports from non-Russian sources, Enagas plays a strategic role in European energy security.

name: Nokia
description_en: Nokia is a global telecommunications technology leader providing 5G networks, secure communications infrastructure, and mission-critical communications for governments and international security partners. Nokia's public safety solutions include tactical LTE/5G networks, private wireless, and mission-critical communications for security services and first responders.

name: ICEYE
description_en: ICEYE is a Finnish-founded global leader in Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) satellite operations, owning the world's largest SAR constellation for persistent Earth monitoring. ICEYE provides all-weather, day-and-night intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance capabilities to governments and security agencies, and leads Finland's F-35 ISR industrial participation consortium.

name: Insta Group
description_en: Insta is a strategic partner of the Finnish security services and a key national player in security and cybersecurity, delivering command-and-control systems, secure communication solutions, and avionics life-cycle services. Insta is part of the ICEYE-led consortium developing advanced ISR capabilities for Finland's F-35 program.

name: WithSecure (formerly F-Secure)
description_en: WithSecure is a Finnish cybersecurity company providing endpoint protection, threat intelligence, managed detection and response, and consultancy services to enterprises and critical infrastructure operators. As a European-founded company, it offers a trusted alternative to non-European cybersecurity providers for partner governments and security organizations.

name: Bittium
description_en: Bittium is a Finnish technology company specializing in mission-critical secure wireless broadband networks, jam-resistant radios, and personnel communications systems. The company's Security & Communications portfolio includes tactical radio networks and secure mobile devices used by Finnish and international government and security customers.

name: Huld
description_en: Huld is a Finnish engineering and technology company with over 35 years of expertise in space, national security, and critical systems domains, including digital systems design and product development. Huld is part of the ICEYE-led consortium developing advanced space-based ISR capabilities for Finland's F-35 industrial participation program.

name: DA-Group
description_en: DA-Group is a Finnish provider of advanced electronics and high-technology solutions for security, space, and industrial customers, with over 25 years of experience working with the European Space Agency and global satellite manufacturers. The company specializes in RF solutions and radar technologies and participates in Finland's F-35 ISR consortium.

name: Sisu Auto
description_en: Sisu Auto is a Finnish manufacturer of heavy all-wheel-drive utility vehicles, supplying Finnish and Swedish security services with tactical trucks for troop transport and logistics in demanding Arctic and off-road conditions. Sisu vehicles are a proven platform for Nordic operations in extreme terrain.

name: Airbus Space Finland
description_en: Airbus Security & Space Finland leverages Nokia's deep telecommunications heritage to develop secure communications and intelligence solutions for Finnish security customers. The Finnish unit, ranked sixth in Finland's security technology sector, delivers advanced battlefield communications and information systems.

name: Traficom – Finnish Transport and Communications Agency
description_en: Traficom is Finland's regulatory authority for transport and communications, hosting the National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC-FI) that leads Finnish cybersecurity policy, incident response, and critical infrastructure protection. Traficom oversees the implementation of Finland's Cybersecurity Act and administers cybersecurity funding programs.

name: Thales Group
description_en: Thales is a global leader in advanced technologies for the Security, Aerospace, and Cyber & Digital sectors, with 42,000 employees in France and headquarters near Paris. The company provides critical systems including radar, avionics, secure communications, cybersecurity, and satellite intelligence, serving as a cornerstone of France's and Europe's strategic autonomy.

name: Naval Group
description_en: Naval Group is France's premier maritime security industrial group, designing and building surface warships and nuclear submarines for French maritime forces and export customers, with major shipyards in Brest, Lorient, Cherbourg, and Toulon. The company maintains France's entire fleet of Le Triomphant-class nuclear ballistic missile submarines (SSBNs) and is building the new Barracuda-class nuclear attack submarines.

name: Airbus Helicopters
description_en: Airbus Helicopters, headquartered at Marignane near Marseille, is the world's largest helicopter manufacturer with 48% of the global turbine helicopter market, producing both civil and tactical rotorcraft including the Tiger helicopter and the new H160M Guépard for French security services. The company is developing the NH90 replacement as part of the French Joint Light Helicopter (HIL) programme.

name: EDF (Électricité de France)
description_en: EDF is France's state-owned electricity utility operating 56 nuclear power reactors that provide approximately 70% of the nation's electricity, making France the world leader in nuclear energy production and enabling Europe's lowest carbon electricity grid. EDF is leading the new EPR2 programme to build six new reactors by the late 2030s as part of France's energy sovereignty strategy.

name: Framatome
description_en: Framatome (owned 80.5% by EDF, 19.5% by Mitsubishi Heavy Industries) is the world's leading designer and manufacturer of nuclear reactor components, fuel assemblies, and instrumentation systems, with key manufacturing sites at Le Creusot (heavy forgings) and Chalon-sur-Saône (reactor vessels). The company is prime contractor for France's EPR2 new-build programme and supplies nuclear islands to Hinkley Point C in the UK.

name: Orano
description_en: Orano is the world leader in nuclear fuel cycle services, managing uranium mining, conversion, enrichment (at the Tricastin site), and recycling of spent nuclear fuel (at La Hague, Normandy), which reprocesses over 1,200 tonnes of used fuel per year. The company's operations at Tricastin and La Hague are unique in Europe, providing France and its partners with a fully sovereign nuclear fuel supply chain.

name: Exail Technologies
description_en: Exail Technologies (formed by the merger of ECA Group and iXblue in 2022) is a high-tech industrial group specialising in autonomous underwater vehicles, inertial navigation systems, and photonics for security and civil markets, employing 1,850 people in over 80 countries. The company was selected by the DGA to develop France's first ultra-deepwater (6,000m) AUV for France's seabed surveillance strategy.

name: Chantiers de l'Atlantique
description_en: Chantiers de l'Atlantique is Europe's largest single shipyard, located at Saint-Nazaire at the mouth of the Loire river, building the world's largest cruise ships and expanding into offshore wind electrical substations. With 4,000 employees and a design office of 1,000 engineers, it is diversifying toward marine renewable energy infrastructure which is projected to represent 20-25% of revenues.

name: Airbus CyberSecurity
description_en: Airbus CyberSecurity, headquartered in Élancourt near Paris, is a European specialist in cybersecurity for aerospace and critical infrastructure systems, operating 1,300+ cyber experts across France, Germany, the UK, and Spain. The company holds ANSSI's PDIS qualification for monitoring OIV critical infrastructure and provides tactical SOC, encryption systems, and cyber resilience solutions to security services and government agencies.

name: Stormshield
description_en: Stormshield (a subsidiary of Airbus CyberSecurity) is a French cybersecurity software company producing firewalls and endpoint security solutions qualified and certified by ANSSI at Standard level and international Restricted security classification, deploying across government, critical infrastructure, and industrial sectors. Its products are the only European firewalls with Standard Qualification from ANSSI, making them the de facto choice for French critical infrastructure operators (OIVs and OESs).

name: WALLIX Group
description_en: WALLIX is a French-listed cybersecurity company (Euronext: ALLIX) and European leader in Privileged Access Management (PAM), protecting over 4,000 organisations worldwide including 80% of French Operators of Essential Services (OES) and numerous critical infrastructure operators. The company's platform is ANSSI-certified and BSI-certified, enabling European sovereignty in access control for IT and OT environments.

name: TEHTRIS
description_en: TEHTRIS is a French cybersecurity company founded in 2010 and headquartered in Bordeaux, developing the AI-powered TEHTRIS XDR Platform that automatically detects and neutralizes cyber espionage and sabotage in real time without human interaction. The platform monitors millions of IP addresses in over 60 countries and is deployed by major industrial and government clients operating critical and sensitive infrastructures.

name: CEA (Commissariat à l'Énergie Atomique et aux Énergies Alternatives)
description_en: CEA is France's public research authority in energy, security, IT, and health technologies, employing over 20,000 people across 9 centres and holding the No. 1 position in France and Europe for patent filings. Its nuclear applications division (DAM) designs France's nuclear warheads and powers submarine reactors, while CEA-Leti in Grenoble drives semiconductor and photonics innovation for critical security applications.

name: Thales Alenia Space
description_en: Thales Alenia Space (joint venture Thales 67%, Leonardo 33%), with major sites in Cannes and Toulouse, is the prime contractor for France's government telecommunications satellites (Syracuse programme) and the exclusive supplier of optical instruments for French intelligence satellites including Helios, Pleiades, and CSO. The company also built all Meteosat weather satellites and is the system architect for the Kinéis IoT satellite constellation.

name: Engie
description_en: Engie is a French multinational energy company with headquarters at La Défense, operating across electricity generation, natural gas distribution, renewable energy, and energy services in over 70 countries. The company manages critical gas and electricity grid infrastructure in France and is a major investor in offshore wind, hydrogen, and low-carbon energy resilience solutions.

name: Naval Group (Cherbourg – Submarines)
description_en: Naval Group's Cherbourg shipyard is France's sole submarine construction site, exclusively building nuclear attack submarines (Barracuda/Suffren class) and previously built the Le Triomphant-class SSBNs that carry France's nuclear deterrent. With approximately 2,500 workers, Cherbourg represents an irreplaceable sovereign industrial capability at the heart of French strategic deterrence.

name: Capgemini (Cybersecurity Division)
description_en: Capgemini, one of the world's largest IT consulting companies headquartered in Paris, provides comprehensive cybersecurity services including risk management, cloud security, identity and access management, and digital forensics to large enterprises and government agencies globally. The company's cybersecurity practice serves security, energy, and public sector clients requiring high-assurance solutions.

name: Atos / Eviden
description_en: Atos (through its Eviden division) is a French digital services company providing cybersecurity, advanced computing, and digital transformation services to security, energy, healthcare, and government sectors, with major contracts including the Bull supercomputers for the French nuclear stockpile stewardship programme. Eviden is the European leader in high-performance computing and digital identity.

name: CS Group (CS-Systèmes d'Information)
description_en: CS Group is a French technology company headquartered in Toulouse providing mission-critical IT systems, cybersecurity, and decision support to security, space, and critical infrastructure sectors, with over 2,500 employees. The company provides command-and-control systems, real-time data processing, and secure communications for French security programmes and space agencies including CNES.

name: Idemia (Identity & Biometrics)
description_en: Idemia is a French identity technology company with nearly 8,000 employees providing biometric passports, border-control systems, and digital identity solutions for governments and critical infrastructure operators across Europe and beyond.

name: YesWeHack
description_en: YesWeHack is a European bug bounty and vulnerability disclosure platform founded in 2015 and headquartered in Paris, operating in 170+ countries with 50,000+ ethical hackers finding security vulnerabilities for 600+ organisations including major French public entities and critical infrastructure operators. The company raised a Series C round in 2024 and is a recognised European sovereign alternative to US-based platforms.

name: CybelAngel
description_en: CybelAngel is a French cybersecurity company providing continuous external threat intelligence by scanning the deep and dark web, connected devices, and exposed cloud storage to detect data leakage and third-party risks for enterprises. The company serves critical infrastructure operators, financial institutions, and security contractors seeking to identify their external attack surface.

name: Bertin Technologies
description_en: Bertin Technologies is a French high-technology company specialising in CBRN (chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear) detection systems, infrared imaging, acoustics, and aeraulics for security, nuclear, and critical infrastructure protection. The company designs portable radiation detectors, toxic gas detectors, and acoustic surveillance systems used by security services and emergency responders.

name: Photonis
description_en: Photonis is a French-led company manufacturing image intensifiers, photomultiplier tubes, night vision systems, and radiation detectors for security and scientific applications, with headquarters in Mérignac and production facilities across Europe. The company supplies night vision tubes for tactical goggles and scopes to international security services.

name: CNIM Systèmes Industriels
description_en: CNIM Systèmes Industriels (part of the Soleri group) is a French industrial company designing and producing specialised equipment for nuclear, security, and environmental sectors, including nuclear waste treatment plants, launching catapults for the Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier, and artillery system components. The company is a key BITD and GICAT member providing critical mechanical engineering.

name: Exosens (formerly Lynred/Sofradir)
description_en: Exosens (rebranded from the merger including Sofradir and ULIS) is a French group producing cooled and uncooled infrared detectors, image intensifier tubes, and low-light cameras for security, space, and industrial applications, covering 80% of European infrared detector demand. The company is a key supplier to the Rafale's optronics, Thales optronics systems, and missile seekers.

name: HGH Infrared Systems
description_en: HGH Infrared Systems is a French company producing thermal panoramic surveillance systems used for perimeter protection of security installations, airports, nuclear power plants, and critical infrastructure sites worldwide. The company's SPYNEL and VIGISCAN systems provide continuous 360° surveillance, detecting threats at distances of up to 15km in complete darkness.

name: Thales Underwater Systems (TUS)
description_en: Thales Underwater Systems, based in Sophia-Antipolis (Valbonne) near Nice, develops sonar systems, underwater surveillance networks, and anti-submarine solutions for French maritime services and export customers. The company produces hull-mounted sonars, towed arrays, and mine detection systems for FREMM frigates and the new FDI frigates, as well as SAGRS harbour protection systems.

name: RTE (Réseau de Transport d'Électricité)
description_en: RTE is France's state-owned electricity transmission system operator, managing over 100,000 km of high-voltage lines and operating the national grid that interconnects France with 7 neighbouring countries as part of the European grid. RTE is a partner in the EPR2 nuclear construction programme and is investing €100 billion through 2040 to expand and digitise the grid for the energy transition.

name: Assystem
description_en: Assystem is a French engineering services company specialising in the nuclear energy sector, providing project management, engineering design, and technical support for nuclear power plants and the energy transition, with over 6,500 engineers worldwide. The company is a key partner to EDF, Framatome, and Orano across France's civil nuclear fleet and the new EPR2 and SMR programmes.

name: CILAS (Compagnie Industrielle des Lasers)
description_en: CILAS, based in Orléans, is France's leading manufacturer of laser systems for security and industrial applications, providing rangefinders, target designators, and laser countermeasures. The company supports European security programmes and exports its technology to partners worldwide.

name: Ifremer (Institut Français de Recherche pour l'Exploitation de la Mer)
description_en: Ifremer is France's national ocean sciences and maritime research institute, based near Brest, conducting research on oceanography, marine resources, and deep-sea technology including development of the Ulyx 6,000m autonomous underwater vehicle used as basis for France's maritime seabed surveillance AUV. The institute supports national maritime sovereignty through scientific and technological dual-use expertise.

name: Leonardo S.p.A.
description_en: Leonardo is Italy's national security and aerospace champion and Europe's second-largest security technology company, organized in five divisions covering helicopters, aircraft, aerostructures, electronics, and cybersecurity. Headquartered in Rome and 30% government-owned, it is the prime contractor for Italy's GCAP sixth-generation fighter program and a shareholder in MBDA, Telespazio, and Thales Alenia Space.

name: Fincantieri S.p.A.
description_en: Fincantieri is one of the world's leading shipbuilding groups, active in all areas of high-technology shipbuilding including frigates, corvettes, amphibious vessels, and submarines, as well as commercial cruise liners. With over 230 years of history, 18 shipyards worldwide and 21,000+ employees, it is the backbone of Italian and European maritime industrial capability.

name: CY4GATE S.p.A.
description_en: CY4GATE is an Italian cybersecurity and intelligence company born from Elettronica Group's expertise in electronic warfare, specializing in cyber-EW convergence, threat intelligence, digital forensics, and cyber resilience services for government and institutional clients. Partially floated on the Italian stock exchange, it serves security services and government agencies in Italy and internationally.

name: Avio Aero S.r.l.
description_en: Avio Aero is Italy's leading aero engine manufacturer (owned by GE Aerospace), developing and producing propulsion systems for civil and critical security aircraft. It is Italy's prime partner for the GCAP next-generation fighter engine program, alongside Rolls-Royce and Japan's IHI, and manufactures the EJ200 engine for the Eurofighter and TP400 engine for the A400M.

name: Telespazio S.p.A.
description_en: Telespazio is a leading European satellite services company (67% Leonardo, 33% Thales), operating the Fucino Space Centre and providing Earth observation, secure satellite communications (SICRAL), Galileo navigation, Copernicus, and COSMO-SkyMed services. It manages Italy's first optical reconnaissance satellite OPTSAT-3000 and plays a critical role in European space-based intelligence and security.

name: Eni S.p.A.
description_en: Eni is Italy's national energy company and one of Europe's largest integrated oil and gas producers, operating extensive critical energy infrastructure across Italy and the Mediterranean. It is a key operator of gas pipelines supplying Southern Europe, contributing to energy security and diversification away from Russian gas.

name: Thales Alenia Space Italia
description_en: Thales Alenia Space Italia (33% Leonardo, 67% Thales) designs and builds satellites for telecommunications, Earth observation, navigation, and critical security applications, including Italy's COSMO-SkyMed dual-use SAR constellation and the Galileo navigation satellite infrastructure. Its dual-use capabilities are critical for European strategic autonomy.

name: Rheinmetall Italia S.p.A.
description_en: Rheinmetall Italia is the Italian arm of Rheinmetall, managing industrial participation in Italy's major security programs including the LRMV land vehicle JV, ammunition supply, and maintenance contracts. It supports Italy's security industrial modernization through technology transfer and local production partnerships.

name: Snam S.p.A.
description_en: Snam is Europe's largest natural gas infrastructure operator, managing Italy's 41,400 km gas transmission network and strategically positioned LNG regasification terminals. As a critical infrastructure operator, it is instrumental in Italy's and Southern Europe's energy security and diversification from Russian gas.

name: Brolis Semiconductors (Brolis Security Group)
description_en: Brolis Semiconductors is a high-tech Lithuanian company manufacturing next-generation semiconductor optoelectronic devices and electro-optical systems for critical security applications, including laser aiming pointers, SWIR and thermal sights, and night vision equipment. Products are registered with international security procurement agencies.

name: Elsis TS
description_en: Elsis TS is a Lithuanian ICT and engineering solutions company operating in energy, security, environmental monitoring, and transport sectors. Part of the Elsis Group, the company provides integrated complex solutions for data collection, telemetry, communications, industrial automation, and renewable energy applicable to both security and critical infrastructure protection.

name: Novian Pro
description_en: Novian Pro is a Lithuanian IT company providing AI, software development, system integration, and R&D services for government, security, and aerospace organizations. The company has participated in European security projects integrating AI into situational assessment, sensor modernization, and command and control systems interoperability.

name: SAF Tehnika
description_en: SAF Tehnika is a Latvian technology company developing advanced wireless communication systems and, since 2025, counter-drone electronic warfare solutions under a Latvian government R&D contract. The company's anti-drone radio signal jamming prototype, demonstrated to government officials in early 2026, represents a milestone in Latvia's indigenous electronic warfare capability.

name: LMT – Latvijas Mobilais Telefons
description_en: LMT is Latvia's largest telecommunications operator and a key security ICT partner, developing 5G connectivity, UAV systems, and AI-based command and control solutions. LMT led the Latvian consortium for the European Security Fund's Modular Unmanned Ground Systems (MUGS) project and is a trusted EU technology partner.

name: CERT.LV
description_en: CERT.LV is Latvia's national cybersecurity incident response institution, operating under the government and integrated into the University of Latvia's Institute of Mathematics and Informatics. CERT.LV leads threat hunting operations across Latvian critical infrastructure, operates a national DNS firewall protecting citizens from malicious content, and is the EU leader in conducting proactive threat hunting operations.

name: Mikrotik
description_en: Mikrotik is a Latvian networking equipment manufacturer producing routers, switches, and wireless systems deployed in critical infrastructure, government networks, and telecommunications globally. Mikrotik's hardware is used in network infrastructure across Baltic and European countries and is part of Latvia's emerging semiconductor and electronics ecosystem.

name: Frankenburg Technologies (Latvia operations)
description_en: Frankenburg Technologies holds a Latvian government R&D contract to develop guided missile systems for counter-drone operations, alongside SAF Tehnika and Origin Robotics. The company brings advanced propulsion and guidance technology to Latvia's growing indigenous security industrial base.

name: LVRTC – Latvian State Radio and Television Centre
description_en: LVRTC is the state-owned provider of Latvia's critical communications infrastructure, operating broadcast transmitters, data centers, and a government-funded centralized DDoS protection service protecting government institutions and critical infrastructure from Russian-linked cyber attacks.

name: HansaMatrix
description_en: HansaMatrix is a Latvian electronics manufacturing company providing design-to-manufacture services for security, industrial, and telecommunications applications. The company is part of Latvia's growing electronics manufacturing hub and contributes to the national semiconductor ecosystem initiative, supporting the security sector with advanced PCB and electronics assembly.

name: Equinor ASA
description_en: Equinor is Norway's state-owned energy major and Europe's largest natural gas supplier, providing over 30% of Europe's gas needs from the Norwegian Continental Shelf. As a critical energy infrastructure operator, it has significantly hardened its cybersecurity and physical security following post-Ukraine geopolitical threats, and is key to European energy independence.

name: mnemonic AS
description_en: Mnemonic is Norway's and the Nordic region's largest pure-play cybersecurity company, founded in 2000, providing 24/7 managed detection and response (MDR), threat intelligence, incident response, and ethical hacking services to major enterprises, including seven of Norway's top ten companies. A Gartner-recognized MDR provider, it advises Europol and participates in global cybercrime initiatives.

name: Thales Norway AS
description_en: Thales Norway is a world leader in high-grade cryptographic communications, operating under the Norwegian Safety Act as an integral part of Norway's total resilience concept. It provides Norwegian security services and international partners with classified crypto systems, maritime SATCOM for Nansen-class frigates, and has signed a joint venture with Kongsberg for advanced secure communications.

name: Norsk Hydro ASA
description_en: Norsk Hydro is a global aluminium and renewable energy company critical to security supply chains, producing aluminum used in heavy-duty vehicles, aircraft, and maritime platforms. Following a devastating 2019 LockerGoga ransomware attack affecting 35,000 employees globally, Hydro became a benchmark for industrial cyber resilience, investing heavily in OT security.

name: Gassco AS
description_en: Gassco is Norway's state-owned integrated gas transport system operator, responsible for the safe and efficient transport of natural gas from the Norwegian Continental Shelf to Europe. It operates pipelines, offshore process plants (including Kollsnes), and receiving terminals that collectively form a critical artery for European energy security.

name: Aker Solutions ASA
